title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
short_title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes permanent rules that stop the federal government from using taxpayer money to pay for abortions or health insurance plans that cover abortions. The only exceptions are when the pregnancy is from rape or incest, or when the mother's life is in danger. The bill also stops federal employees and federal hospitals from providing abortions, and it requires insurance companies to clearly tell customers if their health plans cover abortions and how much extra it costs. These rules would apply to all federal money, including money used by Washington DC.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for co

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law can be called the No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of two thousand twenty five. The law is divided into two main parts. Title One is about prohibiting federally funded abortions. Title Two is about how this applies under the Affordable Care Act (a health insurance law also known as Obamacare).

sections.title: Section 101 Prohibiting Taxpayer Funded Abortions
sections.text: This section adds a new chapter to the United States Code (the main collection of federal laws). The new chapter is called Prohibiting Taxpayer Funded Abortions. Section three hundred one says no federal money can be spent on abortions. Section three hundred two says no federal money can be spent on health insurance plans that cover abortions. Section three hundred three says federal hospitals and federal employees cannot provide abortions as part of their medical services. Section three hundred four says people, organizations, states, and local governments can still buy separate abortion coverage as long as they use their own money and not federal money or matching funds required for federal programs like Medicaid. Section three hundred five says private insurance companies can still offer abortion coverage and states can contract with them as long as no federal money is used. Section three hundred six says this new law does not change any other existing federal laws that already limit abortion funding. Section three hundred seven says this law does not apply to treating medical problems that happen because of an abortion, whether the abortion was legal or not and whether it was federally funded or not. Section three hundred eight creates exceptions to the funding ban. Federal money can be used for abortions when the pregnancy resulted from rape or incest, or when a doctor certifies that the woman will die unless she has an abortion. Section three hundred nine says these rules also apply to Washington D.C. since Congress controls its budget.

sections.title: Section 102 Amendment to Table of Chapters
sections.text: This section adds the new chapter about prohibiting taxpayer funded abortions to the official table of contents for Title One of the United States Code.

sections.title: Section 201 Clarifying Application of Prohibition to Premium Credits and Cost-Sharing Reductions Under ACA
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to prevent people from using federal tax credits to help pay for health insurance plans that cover abortions. The only exceptions are for abortions due to rape, incest, or to save the mother's life, and for treating complications from abortions. People can still buy separate abortion coverage with their own money, but they cannot use federal tax credits to help pay for it. Insurance companies can still offer separate abortion coverage, but the premiums for that coverage cannot be paid with federal tax credits. Small employers also cannot use federal tax credits to help buy health insurance plans that cover abortions for their employees, with the same exceptions. This section also removes certain parts of the Affordable Care Act that conflict with these new rules. These changes apply to health insurance plans that begin after December thirty first two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 202 Revision of Notice Requirements Regarding Disclosure of Extent of Health Plan Coverage of Abortion and Abortion Premium Surcharges
sections.text: This section requires health insurance companies to clearly tell people whether their health plan covers abortions. This information must be provided when someone signs up for the plan and must be prominently displayed in all marketing materials, comparison tools, and benefit summaries. If a health plan covers abortions and charges extra for that coverage (called a surcharge), the insurance company must separately identify and disclose how much that extra charge is whenever they show the plan's premium cost. This requirement starts thirty days after this law is passed.
